markdown:
#  What is the difference between a budget code and a task code? 
##  Answer 
In Procore, a _budget code_ is applied to line items in Procore's Financial Management tools to categorize the costs in your budget. A budget code in Procore consists of two standard segments (_cost code_ and _cost type_) and an optional sub job segment.
In Procore's Resource Tracking tools, a task code combines a cost code and sub job into a single field to simplify time entry in Timesheets and My Time. Data entered for task codes is automatically attributed to the correct cost code and sub job in your budget.
You can also limit the task codes by cost type so that users can only attribute time entry to labor-specific codes. See [Configure Advanced Settings: Company Level Timesheets](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/timesheets-company/tutorials/configure-advanced-settings-company-level-timesheets).
##### Example
In the Project level Timesheets tool, users can select a single task code instead of separately selecting the cost code and sub job.
![Clipboard Ebc59b4586568f8abb97834b37953de50](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/dd53f5568c52bc796568683e96aec165/3afec671aebaede36f0adce537f16f02/clipboard_ebc59b4586568f8abb97834b37953de50.png?fm=webp)_Timesheets with a task code._
![Clipboard E7fa31d1ee76eada5ac415bb16d76075d](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/9d6ac032f2a22ab3b8e53a1f5a321b95/4b19606a5c4d93ca38d33b9ac6d6fd98/clipboard_e7fa31d1ee76eada5ac415bb16d76075d.png?fm=webp)_Timesheets with a_ cost code and sub job.
